rootCause: The next.config.js file uses ES module syntax (export default) but has a .js extension. Next.js 14.x expects CommonJS for .js files, so the config likely fails to load silently. This means images.remotePatterns (which whitelists upload.wikimedia.org and images.unsplash.com) is never applied. The next/image component then rejects all external image URLs with 'unconfigured host' errors, causing broken images across every page that uses next/image with remote URLs.
evidence: next.config.js line 12 uses 'export default' (ESM syntax) while the file is named next.config.js (not .mjs). Next.js 14.x reads .js config files as CommonJS, so 'export default' is ignored.
All image URLs in the codebase are external (Wikimedia Commons or Unsplash). No local images exist except public/assets/homelander-good.png.
next/image component requires remotePatterns to be configured for external domains. Without the config loading, every next/Image with a remote URL fails.
The Card component (components/ui/Card.tsx, line 40) passes imageSrc directly to next/image with fill. HeroSection (components/ui/HeroSection.tsx, line 35) does the same.

Pages affected: sports/page.tsx (HeroSection + Card with athlete images), science/page.tsx (HeroSection + Card + direct Image usage), food/page.tsx (HeroSection + Card with dish images), app/page.tsx (ImageShowcase uses motion.img which bypasses next/image but may still fail if external URLs are blocked).

filesThatNeedChanges.fixOptionA: Rename the file to next.config.mjs (simplest fix, preserves ESM syntax)

imageUrlsPattern.wikimediaCommons: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/... (used in sports, science, food data files)
imageUrlsPattern.unsplash: https://images.unsplash.com/... (used in MotionHero, MotionHighlights, science page)
imageUrlsPattern.local: /assets/homelander-good.png (used in app/leadership/page.tsx line 274, this one works because it is a public folder path)
